perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for ensuring that their individual filings are submitted in a generally timely manner, and for ensuring the information related to their filings is largely complete and reasonably accurate. However, no party guarantees the absolute or total completeness and accuracy of information contained in their own or other parties' filings, and no party is responsible for verifying information provided by others, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the responsibility for completeness and accuracy from a direct responsibility to a more ambiguous 'generally timely' and 'largely complete and reasonably accurate.' Furthermore, it eliminates the clause where a party is responsible if they know or have reason to believe information is inaccurate. This contradicts the premise of joint filing where each party relies on the other's accurate information, creating uncertainty about liability for inaccurate filings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit A JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for ensuring that their individual filings are submitted in a generally timely manner, and for ensuring the information related to their filings is largely complete and reasonably accurate. However, no party guarantees the absolute or total completeness and accuracy of information contained in their own or other parties' filings, and no party is responsible for verifying information provided by others, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement this 14t h day of February 2020. CLARUS IV-A, L.P. By: Clarus IV GP, L.P., its general partner By: Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., its general partner By: Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ John G. Finley Name: John G. Finley Title: Chief Legal Officer /s/ Stephen A. Schwarzman Stephen A. Schwarzman [Galera Therapeutics, Inc. - Joint Filing Agreement]
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the responsibility for completeness and accuracy of filings, shifting from a direct responsibility to a more ambiguous 'generally timely' and 'largely complete and reasonably accurate.' It also eliminates the clause holding a party responsible if they know or have reason to believe information is inaccurate. This directly contradicts the purpose of a joint filing agreement where each party relies on the accuracy of the other's information, as evidenced in the contradicted text, thereby creating uncertainty about liability for inaccurate filings and potentially impacting the enforceability of the joint filing agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of 1 year, automatically renewing for subsequent one-year periods unless revoked by any party hereto with 60 days written notice to the other parties. Revocation will only be effective for the revoking party after the one-year period has ended or been renewed and will apply after the subsequent year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows immediate revocation upon written notice, whereas the modified text imposes a one-year commitment with specific renewal and notice periods. This creates a contradiction because if a party wishes to immediately withdraw as allowed by the original text, they are now bound by the one-year renewal terms of the modified text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit A JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of 1 year, automatically renewing for subsequent one-year periods unless revoked by any party hereto with 60 days written notice to the other parties. perturbation.justification: The original text allows for immediate revocation, while the changed text introduces a one-year renewal period and requires 60 days' notice for revocation, which directly contradicts the initial provision for immediate termination. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the effective date of revocation, leading to potential disputes about obligations and rights. Given the clear conflict in termination terms, a strong in-text contradiction is present.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement must be executed via a single, original document; counterparts and electronic signatures are not permitted.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement allows for execution in counterparts, whereas the changed text mandates a single original document and prohibits counterparts, as well as electronic signatures, directly contradicting the previously stated allowance. This creates a contradiction for parties attempting to execute and deliver the agreement.
